notes: Round A intentionally captures how an initial reviewer might extract broad claims before enforcing quote preservation and claim atomicity.
These items are not formal benchmark tasks.

extractions.broad_claim: Nvidia revenue surged from $26.9B in FY2023 to $60.9B in FY2024, a 125.9% YoY increase.
extractions.issue: Combines two reported values and one derived growth percentage in a single task.

extractions.broad_claim: Consensus expects Nvidia revenue to reach $130.5B in FY2025 and $215.9B in FY2026, implying a 68.3% CAGR through FY2027.
extractions.issue: Mixes forward estimates, consensus source requirement, and CAGR period ambiguity.

extractions.broad_claim: Nvidia EBITDA will climb from $35.6B in FY2024 to $144.6B in FY2026.
extractions.issue: Forecast basis and data source are not identified in the report.

extractions.broad_claim: EBITDA margin improved from 22.2% in FY2023 to 58.4% in FY2024 and will exceed 70% by FY2027.
extractions.issue: Historical margin values and forward-looking margin projection should be split.

extractions.broad_claim: AI accelerator leadership and global AI infrastructure demand are the primary drivers of Nvidia's performance.
extractions.issue: Causal strength is analytical; not directly reducible to deterministic numeric scoring.

extractions.broad_claim: SG&A margin declined from 9% in FY2023 to 2.1% in FY2026, proving operational leverage.
extractions.issue: Round A wording strengthens 'evident' into 'proving'; this changes the report claim.

extractions.broad_claim: Nvidia's EV/EBITDA valuation is high but trending lower from 43.5x in FY2024 to 31.4x in FY2026.
extractions.issue: Requires dated valuation basis and source; likely not eligible for L1/L2 without more provenance.

extractions.broad_claim: Nvidia's premium PE ratio is justified by its dominant competitive position and long-term growth prospects.
extractions.issue: Investment justification requires analytical support, not deterministic scoring.
